questionText: A thief running at 8 km/hr is chased by a policeman whose speed is 10 km/hr. If the thief is 100 metres ahead of the policeman, the time required for the policeman to catch the thief will be

solution.shortcut: Relative speed 2 km/hr; 100 m gap closed in 3 min.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting m to km conversion.

questionText: A thief steals a car at 2.30 p.m. and drives at 60 kmph. Theft discovered at 3 p.m.; owner sets off at 75 kmph. When will the owner overtake the thief?

steps.explanation: Catch-up time = 30/15 = 2 hr after 3 p.m. = 5 p.m.

solution.shortcut: Head-start / relative speed = catch-up time.
solution.commonMistake: Not converting 30-min head start to distance.

questionText: Aryan runs at 40 m/min. Rahul follows after 5 min at 50 m/min. Rahul's dog runs at 60 m/min with Rahul, shuttles between them until Rahul catches Aryan. Total distance covered by dog is

solution.shortcut: Dog runs for catch-up duration; d = dog speed * time.
solution.commonMistake: Summing infinite shuttle legs instead of using total time.

questionText: A thief was 100 m ahead of a policeman. Ratio of policeman to thief speed = 5:4. How far did the thief run before being caught?

solution.shortcut: Thief distance = (4/(5-4)) * 100 = 400 m.
solution.commonMistake: Reporting policeman's distance (500) instead of thief's (400).

questionText: A walks at 4 km/hr; 4 hours after A's start, B bicycles at 10 km/hr. How far from the starting point does B catch A?

steps.explanation: Relative speed = 10-4 = 6 km/hr. Catch time = 16/6 = 8/3 hr.

solution.shortcut: Catch-up time = head-start / relative speed; distance = faster speed * time.
solution.commonMistake: Using A's distance instead of B's.

questionText: A passenger train (80 kmph) starts 6 hours after a goods train. Passenger overtakes goods after 4 hours. Speed of goods train is

solution.shortcut: Goods distance (10x) = passenger distance (4*80-4x) at overtake.
solution.commonMistake: Using wrong time intervals for each train.

questionText: An athlete's timing should be corrected: starting gun fired 10 m away; timekeeper stands near gun. Error in seconds (speed of sound = 300 m/s) is

solution.shortcut: Direct: t = d/v.
solution.commonMistake: Using 100 m instead of 10 m.

questionText: Sound at 300 m/s. One person hears a sound after 5 sec; another after 6 sec. Maximum and minimum distance between them is

solution.shortcut: Max = sum of distances; min = difference.
solution.commonMistake: Only considering one configuration.

questionText: Two guns fired from same place at 8-min interval. A man approaching hears them 5 min 52 sec apart. Sound speed = 330 m/s. Man's speed (km/hr) is

solution.shortcut: Man's dist in observed interval = sound's dist in (gun interval - observed interval).
solution.commonMistake: Using 480 s instead of 8 s for sound.

questionText: Two cyclists start from same place in opposite directions at 18 kmph (N) and 20 kmph (S). Time to be 47.5 km apart is

solution.shortcut: Separation rate = sum of speeds (opposite directions).
solution.commonMistake: Using difference instead of sum of speeds.

questionText: A and B are stations 10 km apart. P starts from A at 3 km/hr towards B; Q from B at 2 km/hr towards A. Where and when do they meet?
options: A) After 2 hours, 6 km from A
B) After 3 hours, 9 km from A
C) After 2.5 hours, 7.5 km from A
D) After 2 hours, 4 km from A
correctAnswer: A) After 2 hours, 6 km from A

solution.shortcut: Meeting time = total distance / sum of speeds.
solution.commonMistake: Reporting Q's distance from B instead of from A.

questionText: Cars X and Y start from A and B (700 km apart) at 9 a.m. at 60 km/hr each. X stops 10-11 a.m. They cross each other at

solution.shortcut: Subtract stoppage from effective travel time.
solution.commonMistake: Not subtracting the 1-hr stop from X.

questionText: Train A at 48 km/hr from station A. 45 min later, train B at 50 km/hr from B towards A. Distance AB = 232 km. Distance from A at meeting is

solution.shortcut: Set combined distance = 232 accounting for 45-min delay.
solution.commonMistake: Ignoring the 45-min delay.

questionText: Jogging track circumference = 726 m. Deepak and wife start from same point in opposite directions at 4.5 km/hr and 3.75 km/hr. First meeting time is

solution.shortcut: t = circumference / (sum of speeds).
solution.commonMistake: Not converting km/hr to m/hr.

questionText: A (2 rounds/hr) and B (3 rounds/hr) walk circular track from same point at 8 a.m. in opposite directions. Crossings before 9:30 a.m. are

steps.explanation: Relative speed = 5 rounds/hr. Time = 1.5 hr. Crossings = floor(5*1.5) = 7.

solution.shortcut: Crossings = floor(relative speed * time).
solution.commonMistake: Rounding 7.5 up to 8 instead of floor.

questionText: Two cyclists on circular track (circumference 300 m) start from same point in opposite directions at 7 m/s and 8 m/s. Time to meet at starting point is

solution.shortcut: LCM of individual round times.
solution.commonMistake: Confusing first general meeting with meeting at starting point.

modelName: Circular Track Meet at Starting Point

questionText: Two trains 425 km apart move towards each other at 200 km/hr each, halving speed every 0.5 hr. Distance between them after 1.5 hr is

solution.shortcut: Add combined distances per halved-speed interval.
solution.commonMistake: Not halving both trains' speeds each interval.

questionText: P and Q are 120 km apart. A starts from P at 6 a.m.; B from Q at 11 a.m. A is 50% faster than B. They cross at 8 p.m. At what time does A reach destination Q?

steps.explanation: A covers 120 km at 6 km/hr = 20 hr after 6 a.m. = 2 a.m. next day.

solution.shortcut: Find speed from meeting equation; compute journey time.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting A's 5-hr head start on B.

questionText: P and Q are 120 km apart. A from P at 6 a.m.; B from Q at 11 a.m. A 50% faster. Cross at 8 p.m. How many more hours than A does B take to reach destination?

steps.explanation: A speed=6, B speed=4. A time=120/6=20 hr; B time=120/4=30 hr. Diff=10 hr.

solution.shortcut: Compute individual journey times and subtract.
solution.commonMistake: Using relative speed instead of individual times.

questionText: P and Q are 120 km apart. A from P at 6 a.m.; B from Q at 11 a.m. A is 50% faster. Cross at 8 p.m. A's speed (km/hr) is

solution.shortcut: Direct from meeting equation.
solution.commonMistake: Reporting B's speed (4) instead of A's (6).

questionText: Train X leaves New York at 1 A.M. at x mph. Train Z leaves at 2 A.M. eastward. Constant speed for Z to catch X at exactly 5:30 A.M. is

solution.shortcut: Speed of Z = X's distance / Z's travel time.
solution.commonMistake: Using X's total time instead of Z's travel time.

questionText: Two ladies leave cities A and B (24 km apart) simultaneously towards each other. First is 2 km/hr faster and reaches B 1 hr before second reaches A. Each lady's speed is

solution.shortcut: Set up time-difference equation; solve quadratic.
solution.commonMistake: Reversing which lady is faster.

questionText: Buses from terminal at 20 km/hr every 10 min. A man from opposite direction meets buses every 8 min. His speed is

solution.shortcut: Gap = (v_bus + v_man) * meeting interval.
solution.commonMistake: Using same-direction formula for opposite direction.

questionText: Two men at R and S, 76 km apart. R walks 4.5 km/hr uniform; S walks in AP: 3.75, 4, 4.25,... km/hr. They meet x km nearer R than S in integral hours. x is

steps.explanation: R covers 9n/2 km in n hr. S's AP sum = n(n+29)/8.
R covers 36 km; S covers 40 km. x = 40-36 = 4.

solution.shortcut: Use AP sum formula; solve quadratic for integer n.
solution.commonMistake: Not verifying n is an integer.

questionText: Two planes on circle (circumference 1.2 km). Opposite directions: meet every 15 sec. Same direction: overtake every 60 sec. Slower plane speed is

solution.shortcut: Two equations from intervals; solve simultaneously.
solution.commonMistake: Mixing km and m without unit conversion.

questionText: Two cyclists k km apart. Same direction: together in r hr. Opposite: pass in t hr. Ratio of faster to slower speed is

solution.shortcut: Eliminate k by dividing the two equations.
solution.commonMistake: Swapping r and t in the ratio.

questionText: Bus left X for Y; car left 2 hr later, arrived same time. Opposite-direction simultaneous start: they meet after 4/3 hr. Bus travel time X to Y is

solution.shortcut: Set meeting condition; solve quadratic.
solution.commonMistake: Inverting 4/3 to 3/4.

questionText: Two trains from stations 200 km apart, opposite directions, simultaneous start, cross 110 km from one station. Ratio of speeds is

steps.explanation: One covers 110 km; other covers 90 km in same time. Ratio = 110:90 = 11:9.

solution.shortcut: Speed ratio = distance ratio at simultaneous-start meeting.
solution.commonMistake: Comparing 110 to 200 instead of to 90.

questionText: Two trains toward each other at 50 kmph and 60 kmph. At meeting, second had travelled 120 km more. Distance A to B is

solution.shortcut: Equal time => x/v1 = (x+120)/v2; solve for x.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to add both trains' distances.

questionText: Train A from Ludhiana at 11 a.m. at 60 km/hr. Train B from Ludhiana at 2 p.m. at 72 km/hr. They meet at

steps.explanation: A's head start when B departs = 60*3 = 180 km.
Relative speed = 12 km/hr. Catch time = 15 hr after 2 p.m. = 5 a.m. next day.

solution.shortcut: Head-start / relative speed = catch-up time.
solution.commonMistake: Computing head start from 11 a.m.

questionText: Train M: X to Y in 4 hr, leaves 5 a.m. Train N: Y to X in 3.5 hr, leaves 7 a.m. They cross each other at

solution.shortcut: Express journey fractions; set sum = 1.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting M's 2-hr head start before N departs.

questionText: Train A (60 km/hr) leaves Mumbai at 6 p.m. Train B (90 km/hr) leaves Mumbai at 9 p.m. Train C leaves Delhi at 9 p.m. All meet same time. Delhi-Mumbai = 1260 km. Speed of C is

steps.explanation: C covers 1260-540=720 km in 6 hr => 120 km/hr.

solution.shortcut: Find meeting time from A-B condition; compute C's speed.
solution.commonMistake: Not accounting for A's 3-hr head start.

questionText: Amit walks A to B and drives back: 6 hr 45 min total. Driving both ways: 2 hr less. Walking both ways takes

steps.explanation: Drive both = 27/4 - 2 = 19/4 hr. Drive one way = 19/8 hr.
Walk one way = 27/4-19/8 = 35/8 hr. Walk both = 35/4 = 8h45m.

solution.shortcut: Walk both = 2*total - drive both.
solution.commonMistake: Not halving drive-both to get drive-one-way.

questionText: Reena arrived at 6:30 p.m. (30 min early) and walked home. Father left at usual time, picked her up on way, arrived home 10 min early. How long did Reena walk?

steps.explanation: 10 min saved = 5 min each way; father met Reena at 6:55 p.m.

solution.shortcut: Time saved = 2*(one-way avoided); Reena walked during early-arrival window.
solution.commonMistake: Using full 10 min as walking time.

questionText: Mr X arrived 2 hr early; walked home at 4 mph. Met coachman on way; arrived 1 hr before schedule. Distance house to station is

steps.explanation: 1 hr saved => 0.5 hr less each way => X walked 1.5 hr.
Walk dist = 4*1.5 = 6 miles. Coach covered 6 miles in 0.5 hr => speed = 12 mph.

solution.shortcut: Time saved=1 hr => 0.5 hr less each way.
solution.commonMistake: Confusing total time saved with one-way time saved.

questionText: Cat in tunnel AB at 3/8 from entrance A. If cat runs to A, train catches it at A; if to B, catches it at B. Ratio train speed to cat speed is

steps.explanation: Let AB=1. Cat at 3/8 from A. Scenario 1: cat runs 3/8 to A; train reaches A in same time.
In time cat ran 3/8, train at A. Then train covers 1 while cat covers BD=1/4. Ratio=4.

solution.shortcut: Apply both scenario constraints; BD=1-3/8-3/8=1/4; ratio=4.
solution.commonMistake: Not applying both scenarios simultaneously.

questionText: Engine speed 42 km/hr empty; 24 km/hr with 9 compartments. Reduction proportional to sqrt(compartments). Maximum compartments is

steps.explanation: 42-6*sqrt(n)=0 => n=49. Answer is 49, none of options A-C.

solution.shortcut: Find k; set speed=0 to get max n.
solution.commonMistake: Choosing 48 instead of recognising correct answer is 49.

questionText: Ram (5 km/hr) starts from A at 9 a.m.; Shyam (10 km/hr) from A at 9:45 a.m. AB=5 km; both run to B and return. First meeting time is

steps.explanation: Ram reaches B at 10 a.m. Shyam at 2.5 km from A.
Relative speed = 15 km/hr. Gap=2.5 km. t=10 min. Meet at 10:10 a.m.

solution.shortcut: Find positions at Ram's turning point; use relative speed.
solution.commonMistake: Not accounting for Shyam's 45-min delay.

questionText: Ram (5 km/hr) from A at 9 a.m.; Shyam (10 km/hr) from A at 9:45 a.m. AB=5 km; both run to B and return. Shyam overtakes Ram at

steps.explanation: Shyam at B at 10:15. Ram at 15/4 km from A. Gap=5/4 km; both to A.
Relative speed=5 km/hr. t=(5/4)/5=15 min. 10:15+15=10:30 a.m.

solution.shortcut: Positions when Shyam at B; same-direction catch-up.
solution.commonMistake: Using opposite-direction relative speed (15) instead of same-direction (5).

questionText: Man walks up moving-up escalator in 30 sec; walks down same escalator in 90 sec. Time to walk up stationary escalator is

solution.shortcut: Add both equations to find man's speed alone.
solution.commonMistake: Confusing down-moving-up as x+y instead of x-y.

questionText: Hare is 60 hare-leaps ahead of hound. Per 3 hound-leaps, hare takes 4 leaps. Hare leap=7/4 m; hound leap=11/4 m. Hound overtakes in how many leaps?

steps.explanation: Per 3 hound leaps: hound=33/4 m; hare=7 m. Gain=5/4 m.

solution.shortcut: Convert head start to metres; find gain per leap set; divide.
solution.commonMistake: Using hare leaps without converting to distance.

questionText: Arun rode 1/3 of trip; walked 2/3. Walking time = 20 * riding time. Ratio riding speed to walking speed is

solution.shortcut: Ratio = (ride dist/ride time) / (walk dist/walk time).
solution.commonMistake: Inverting the ratio.

questionText: Car overtakes bus at 4:30 p.m. Car reaches Jaipur at 6:00 p.m. After 1-hr stop, car returns; meets bus at 7:30 p.m. (bus still towards Jaipur). Bus arrival at Jaipur is

steps.explanation: Car covers x km in 1.5 hr. Car speed = 2x/3.
Car leaves Jaipur at 7 p.m.; drove 0.5 hr back = x/3 km. Bus x/3 km from Jaipur at 7:30.
Bus: 3 hr covered 2x/3 km => speed=2x/9. Remaining x/3 at 2x/9 = 3/2 hr. Arrival=9:00 p.m.

solution.shortcut: Car back travel=0.5 hr from 7 p.m.; compute bus speed then remaining time.
solution.commonMistake: Not noting car departed Jaipur at 7 p.m. (after 1-hr stop).

questionText: Karan covers a distance at 60 kmph in 9 hr. Time at 90 kmph is

solution.commonMistake: Multiplying instead of dividing by speed ratio.

questionText: Speed of bus = 72 kmph. Distance covered in 5 sec is

solution.shortcut: d = v(km/hr) * (5/18) * t(sec).
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to convert km/hr to m/s.
